raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/XXXX my American Express Card was charged by XXXX ( my business website host ) for {$460.00}. I called and stated that that was too much money and I wanted to close the account. They offered me a 20 % discount and I accepted that. They then charged my card {$370.00}, the agreed upon price. After a few days of not getting the original refund of {$460.00} I called XXXX why I didn't receive a refund and was told the charge was never put through. When I got my Amex bill, it showed 2 charges, one for {$460.00} and one for {$370.00}, both charges. After trying multiple times to resolve with XXXX I called Amex and disputed the charge of {$460.00}. After investigating I was given that amount back and the merchant kept the agreed upon {$370.00}. Fast forward to XX/XX/XXXX. I get a notification from XXXX that my website is being suspended and deleted for non payment. When I contacted them they claimed I disputed the {$370.00} and owed them that money. I contacted Amex and they confirmed that I never disputed that amount and the only amount returned to me was the overcharge of {$460.00}. After dozens of calls and emails to XXXX they said the issue was with Amex and not them. They then told me that to keep from deleting my websites I had to immediately pay {$190.00}. I paid that money ( which I consider to be extortion ) in order to get my business back up and running. I called Amex again, explained the situation and was told to move to another vendor and they would charge back the {$190.00} and {$370.00}. I them moved my websites to a new host. I called Amex back and they now said they couldn't charge back the {$370.00} because it was past 60 days. I paid for 3 years of hosting service and got 7 months worth. I asked them to contact the merchant on my behalf and they said that wasn't possible, that they agreed I was duped but that they couldn't do anything about it. I explained that this was an error on their part according to the vendor and they denied have made the chargeback of {$370.00} and again, told me to contact XXXX. I contacted XXXX again via email and was told by their escalation division that the error was Amex 's and I needed to take it up with them. I spoke to escalation and Amex and they claimed they can't do anything because they aren't allowed to contact merchant 's directly to sort the situation and the computer system doesn't allow for disputes past 60 days. I am at XXXX XXXX  end trying to resolve this. Both XXXX and Amex claim the other made a mistake. All I know is I'm currently out {$370.00} and XXXX for services I never received.
